codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning, session 019e5571) confirmed NEW-OBJ-1 is resolved. Raised 4 new findings with PROPOSED_FIX entries and 2 observations. CF-1: AC6/Technical Hints drift — unconditional mkdir -p /var/tmp/codex-outputs before branch in hint conflicts with AC6 only-in-fallback text. CF-2: AC4 substring verifier is over-permissive vs AC4 text exact-forms requirement. CF-3: AC5 verifier does not compare Step 2 blocks between the two files. CF-4: AC3 JSON must_match checks '..' rejection but not '.' rejection. OBS-5: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD} in _base is not a tee target, acceptable. OBS-6: M4 adequately prohibits tee via _base variable. Codex verdict: 'A narrow PASS on that single objection is defensible. A strict /close BA-validation PASS is weaker unless you either fix or explicitly waive the AC3/AC4/AC5/AC6 verifier/hint drift above.'
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: CF-1 through CF-4 each carry PROPOSED_FIX entries. QA classified each per R6 rule: CF-4 (AC3 missing dot check), CF-3 (AC5 no comparison), and CF-2 (AC4 substring over-permissive) are R6-blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift. CF-1 (AC6 hint conflict) is also blocking — the normative hint contradicts AC6 text and would cause a false verification fail. OBS-5 and OBS-6 accepted as non-blocking observations.

prior_objection_verification.original_claim: AC4 text permitted Form 2 (${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt) as an allowed tee target but the AC4 verifier grep pattern CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex did not match it.
prior_objection_verification.evidence: ticket-20260521-090100.md AC4 (Revised 2026-05-22T09:30:00Z) now lists exactly two allowed forms: (1) $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt and (2) /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-$$-$(date +%s).txt. Form 2 (${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/docs/codex/...) is absent from the ticket. acceptance-criteria-20260521-090100.json AC4 verifier grep pattern CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex|/var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output matches both listed allowed forms. Text and verifier are internally consistent. No Form 2 or CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:- string present in acceptance-criteria JSON.

new_objections.description: AC3 verifier cannot verify the standalone '.' guard that AC3 text requires.
new_objections.detail: AC3 text requires: 'the guard explicitly rejects . and .. as standalone values.' The acceptance-criteria-20260521-090100.json AC3 must_match pattern for this guard is a regex that matches lines containing != ".." or != '..' but does NOT match lines containing != "." (single dot). If dev implements only a != ".." guard without a != "." guard, the AC3 verifier PASSES even though AC3 text requires both. This is spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the AC literal text mandates two standalone rejection checks but the verifier recipe can only confirm the '..' check is present.
files_affected: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260521-090100.json (AC3 must_match — pattern covers '..' but not standalone '.')
new_objections.proposed_fix: Add an additional must_match entry to AC3 JSON check that requires evidence of '.' (single dot) standalone rejection, e.g. matching '!= "."' or '!= \'.\'' in the guard block. The AC3 ticket text should also explicitly call out that both guards are independently verifiable. (Codex CF-4 PROPOSED_FIX)

new_objections.description: AC5 verifier does not actually compare Step 2 blocks between commands/codex.md and SKILL.md, contrary to AC5 literal text.
new_objections.detail: AC5 text states: 'WHEN the Step 2 path-resolution block is compared to commands/codex.md Step 2 / THEN both contain the same path-safety guard and same branch structure.' The acceptance-criteria JSON AC5 check uses three independent grep patterns against SKILL.md only (dot-dot guard present, Source: cross-link, docs/codex path). None of these checks compare SKILL.md to commands/codex.md. A dev could implement SKILL.md with a structurally different Step 2 block that still passes all three grep checks while diverging from codex.md. Verifier recipe performs no comparison as AC5 text mandates.
files_affected: docs/dev/ticket-20260521-090100.md (AC5 text: both contain the same path-safety guard and same branch structure)
docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260521-090100.json (AC5 check: three grep patterns against SKILL.md only, no comparison to codex.md)
new_objections.proposed_fix: Update AC5 JSON check to add a Python script that extracts the Step 2 fenced bash block from both commands/codex.md and SKILL.md and asserts structural or textual equality. (Codex CF-3 PROPOSED_FIX)

new_objections.description: AC4 verifier is a substring grep that can pass implementations with malformed tee targets; AC4 text requires 'exactly these forms'.
new_objections.detail: AC4 text says 'every physical tee target in both files is one of these hook-resolvable forms: [Form 1] or [fallback literal].' The AC4 JSON verifier (checks 3 and 4) filters out lines containing the substring CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex and passes them. A tee target like tee "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/wrong_file.log" contains the substring and passes the verifier even though it violates AC4 text. Codex independently confirmed: the verifier enforces 'must contain one of these substrings' rather than 'must be exactly one of these forms'. Spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC4 says exact forms; verifier allows any target containing the substring.
files_affected: docs/dev/ticket-20260521-090100.md (AC4 text: every physical tee target is one of these hook-resolvable forms)
new_objections.proposed_fix: Replace the substring grep approach in AC4 checks 3 and 4 with a Python script that extracts actual tee target tokens from each line and asserts each token is exactly one of the two allowed forms (using regex tee\s+"([^"]+)" to extract targets). (Codex CF-2 PROPOSED_FIX)

new_objections.description: Technical Hints Step 2 block shows unconditional mkdir -p /var/tmp/codex-outputs before the branch, directly contradicting AC6 (only-in-fallback).
new_objections.detail: AC6 text says '/var/tmp/codex-outputs/ appears ONLY in the else/fallback branch (not in the canonical if-branch) in BOTH files.' The Technical Hints path-resolution block (ticket lines 202-221) shows 'mkdir -p /var/tmp/codex-outputs' as the first line, unconditionally before the if/else branch. A dev following this hint would produce code where /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ appears before the branch — violating AC6 text. The AC6 verifier (must_contain_as_fallback_only) is correctly specified but the normative hint contradicts it. This is a hint-vs-AC-text conflict in the spec document that would cause a false verification fail for a compliant dev following the hint.
files_affected: docs/dev/ticket-20260521-090100.md (Technical Hints line 204 unconditional mkdir -p /var/tmp/codex-outputs; AC6 lines 186-193 only-in-fallback requirement)
new_objections.proposed_fix: Move 'mkdir -p /var/tmp/codex-outputs' from the top of the Step 2 hint block into the else branch (just before the fallback echo statement), so the hint is consistent with AC6. (Codex CF-1 PROPOSED_FIX)

codex_observations_accepted.description: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD} in _base does not recreate NEW-OBJ-1 because _base is used only for mkdir/echo in Step 2, not as a tee target. If dev used tee "$_base/...", AC4's grep would correctly catch it since the string lacks the allowed substrings. Non-blocking.
tee "$_base/..." is not literally tee $SINGLEVAR as M4 defines, but M4's exact-allowed-target list still prohibits it. AC4 is adequate for this misuse case. Non-blocking.

evidence_quality.notes: All factual claims retain file-read evidence from prior iterations. NEW-OBJ-1 resolution verified by grep: Form 2 absent from ticket and JSON. No new evidence-quality issues.

scope_alignment.notes: Scope unchanged. User requirement (terminate all subagent no-tee workarounds) covered by all 6 tee sites, all 11 roles. Won't Have exclusions unchanged and authorized.

investigation_completeness.notes: No new investigation requirements from iteration 3. Prior ticket reconciliation, role grep, root cause verification carried forward.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: All three affected files confirmed with correct content per prior iterations. Iteration 3 changes confined to AC4 text and JSON file.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: Four new blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift objections: NEW-OBJ-2 (AC3 missing '.' guard verification), NEW-OBJ-3 (AC5 no block comparison), NEW-OBJ-4 (AC4 substring over-permissive), NEW-OBJ-5 (AC6 hint/text conflict). Per R6 rule (task 20260519-211515, dual-anchor #1 and #2), each is a blocking BA-validation objection that must not be downgraded.

summary: FAIL (iteration 3). Prior blocking objection NEW-OBJ-1 (AC4 Form 2 spec_text_vs_execution_drift) is fully resolved. However, Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.5, xhigh, session 019e5571) surfaced four new blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift issues: (1) AC3 JSON verifier cannot confirm standalone '.' rejection — only checks '..'; (2) AC5 verifier performs no comparison between SKILL.md and commands/codex.md Step 2 blocks; (3) AC4 verifier uses substring grep that cannot enforce exact-form compliance; (4) Technical Hints show unconditional mkdir -p /var/tmp/codex-outputs before the branch, directly contradicting AC6's only-in-fallback requirement. All four are verifier or guidance conflicts with AC literal text per R6. None require deep rework — each has a concrete targeted fix. BA should resolve all four in iteration 4.
verdict_reasoning: NEW-OBJ-1 resolved. Four new R6-blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift issues (NEW-OBJ-2 through NEW-OBJ-5) force FAIL per R6 rule. Codex independently confirmed these are real verifier gaps with concrete PROPOSED_FIX entries.
